body:
## Why

The repo was missing the last two files needed for the documented Claude Code + Codex integration "final shape". Direct inspection showed the rest of the checklist was already satisfied on the branch base — `AGENTS.md`, `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.txt`, `scripts/check_env_contract.py`, `.claudeignore`, and the `.claude/`, `.codex/`, `.cursor/` agent configs all already exist. Codex needs no new repo file (it reads `AGENTS.md`).

Only two files were genuinely absent — this PR adds them.

## What

- **`CLAUDE.md`** (new, repo root) — a lean (~250 words, under the repo's own <500-token hygiene rule in `token-optimizer/02-claude-md/`) context lookup table. Points to `AGENTS.md` as the source of truth, lists the doctrine guard tests (non-negotiables), and uses **corrected** paths/commands vs. the older template (`api/routers/` not `api/routes/`, `core/config/`, `APP_ENV=test pytest`, real `make` targets). Every path and test it references was verified to exist.
- **`.github/workflows/claude-code.yml`** (new) — `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` that responds to `@claude` on issues, issue comments, PR review comments, and PR reviews. Authenticates via a **GitHub App token** (`actions/create-github-app-token@v2` from `APP_ID` + `APP_PRIVATE_KEY`) plus `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. Mirrors `ci.yml` conventions (name, concurrency, timeout).

## Required repo secrets (set in Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions)

The workflow only runs green once these exist:

- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
- `APP_ID`
- `APP_PRIVATE_KEY` (GitHub App with Contents / Issues / Pull requests = Read & Write)

Setting secrets and installing the GitHub App are repo-settings actions performed in the GitHub UI — they cannot be committed.

## Smoke test (after merge + secrets configured)

Open an issue titled **"Claude smoke test"** containing:

> `@claude افحص الريبو بدون تعديل. قل لي فقط هل CLAUDE.md و AGENTS.md و GitHub Actions موجودة، وما هي أعلى 3 مخاطر P0.`

Expected: the **Claude Code** workflow starts a run and Claude replies in the issue. The run should go green.

## Verification done

- `claude-code.yml` parses as valid YAML.
- All paths and the six skill files referenced by `CLAUDE.md` exist.
- All six doctrine guard tests referenced by `CLAUDE.md` exist.
- Founder checklist now reports `OK` for `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.github/workflows/claude-code.yml`.

## Pre-existing CI failures (NOT caused by this PR)

Two checks from `.github/workflows/repository-hardening.yml` (untouched by this PR) fail on every PR for environmental reasons — by maintainer decision they are left as-is here:

- **Gitleaks secret scan** — `gitleaks-action@v2` requires a paid `GITLEAKS_LICENSE` org secret (`[Dealix-sa] is an organization. License key is required`).
- **Trivy filesystem scan** — `aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0` is pinned to a tag that no longer resolves.

These are tracked separately from this change.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JtuQhjxPpsGxUA95rhP6kk

commits.messageBody:
- CLAUDE.md: lean (<500 token) context lookup table pointing to AGENTS.md as
  the source of truth, with corrected paths/commands and the doctrine guards.
- .github/workflows/claude-code.yml: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1 that
  responds to @claude on issues and PRs, authenticating via a GitHub App token
  (APP_ID + APP_PRIVATE_KEY) plus ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JtuQhjxPpsGxUA95rhP6kk
commits.messageHeadline: Add CLAUDE.md and Claude Code GitHub Action workflow

title: Add CLAUDE.md and Claude Code GitHub Action workflow
